name: hESC-EPC conditioned medium improved human skin-aging measures
summary: A randomized split-face study reported better pigmentation and wrinkle measures when conditioned medium was added to microneedling.
statement: In 25 women, microneedling plus hESC-derived endothelial precursor cell conditioned medium improved physician-rated pigmentation and wrinkles plus Mexameter and Visiometer measures versus microneedling plus saline.
population_or_model: Women with facial skin-aging features in a randomized controlled split-face study
time_horizon: Five treatment sessions repeated at 2-week intervals
quantitative_note: Reported comparisons favored conditioned medium for pigmentation and wrinkles with p < 0.05; one participant had mild desquamation.
caveats: Skin appearance and instrumented skin measures are aging-relevant biomarkers, not systemic healthspan or functional endpoints.
The intervention was combined with microneedling, so the conditioned-medium contribution is adjunctive.
The study was small and limited to short-term dermatologic outcomes.
